source: Incus Server - 5 Setting Up and Managing Images: Finding images and creating, starting, renaming and listing containers

questions.q: List all available images from the images: remote (pipe through more).
questions.why: incus image list images: shows the (large) catalogue of container/VM images.

questions.q: How do you narrow the image list to just Rocky Linux options?
options: incus image list images: | grep rocky
questions.why: Pipe the listing through grep rocky for a manageable list.

questions.q: Which command creates and launches a Rocky Linux 8 container named rockylinux-test-8?
questions.why: incus launch images:<image> <name> creates and starts the container.

questions.q: After `incus launch`, what is the container's state?
options: Running -- it starts automatically when created

questions.q: How do you rename a container (e.g. rockylinux-test-8 to rockylinux-8)?
options: Stop it first, then: incus move rockylinux-test-8 rockylinux-8
questions.why: Stop the container, then use incus move <old> <new>.

questions.q: List your containers (name, state, IP, type, snapshots).
questions.why: incus list shows all your instances and their addresses.
